prompt: My MCP server isn't connecting. I need to check what processors are running in workspace prod-streams (project 65094f059e0776665611598b) and see the pipeline definition for a specific one.

assertions.name: correct_list_command
assertions.description: Uses 'atlas streams processors list' with correct subcommand structure
assertions.reason: Common errors: 'atlas stream' (singular), 'atlas streams list' (missing 'processors'), wrong verb

assertions.name: uses_instance_flag
assertions.reason: The flag name is --instance, not --workspace. Using the wrong flag causes CLI errors.

assertions.name: shows_describe_command
assertions.description: Shows 'atlas streams processors describe <name> --instance prod-streams' for pipeline details

correct_commands.note: The workspace is passed via --instance flag, not --workspace

common_mistakes.fix: Atlas Stream Processing workspaces are called 'instances' in the CLI. Use --instance.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using 'get' or 'show' instead of 'describe' for pipeline details
common_mistakes.fix: Use 'atlas streams processors describe <name>' for full processor details including pipeline

skill_reference: references/atlas-cli-streams.md: full CLI command reference with --instance flag syntax
